title: elymus zejensis
intro: Elymus zejensis, also known as Zhima Qiang or Chinese Wild Rye, is a type of perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of China and Central Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various health conditions. The health benefits of Elymus zejensis include reducing fever, relieving coughs and respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. In herbal preparations, Elymus zejensis is often used in the form of tea, infusions, or as a component in traditional Chinese medicine formulations.
